endpoints.name: Final Fantasy Brave Exvius opening-arc party (Rain, Lasswell, Fina)

description: Oh sir, terribly sorry to bother you, Edgelord wanted a clean 1:1 edge and I do apologize — but the trio cohort really does anchor better as a node than as three pairwise edges. Verified on disk: N=3 distinct FFBE characters across 5 printings, all Opus VIII, all triviabot-passed, all with the Square Enix press-release citation framing them as the simultaneous-debut opening-arc party (Category BE). Mirrors the wave-135 ff6-returners-party node exactly. The Fina 8-061C trivia even has a dedicated 'Trio context' bullet that names the trio outright. Made the node, no rebellion-hub adjacency asserted (Potion sub-rule 2 — FFBE's canonical event-structure is JRPG-protagonist-trinity, not Returners-style anti-imperial-extraction). Hope that's alright.
citations: [Square Enix press release: Opus VIII announcement, na.finalfantasy.com/news/1043, 2019-03-22]
[FFTCG Crystarium: How to Win Limited Opus VIII Review]

verification_log.claim_to_verify: N=3 unique FFBE characters in corpus, all Opus VIII, all with explicit trio-framing in trivia bodies
verification_log.evidence: 5 card files exist on disk (Rain 8-021R, Lasswell 8-043H + foil duplicate, Fina 8-060L Legend + 8-061C Common). All 5 have ip_verified: true with FFBE attribution. Rain trivia: 'Opus VIII brought Final Fantasy Brave Exvius to the forefront with multiple new characters, Rain among them' + 'Rain and Lasswell ... canonical FFBE protagonist pair debuted together in FFTCG'. Lasswell trivia: Square Enix press release explicitly names him as 'brought to the forefront' co-headliner. Fina 8-061C trivia: dedicated 'Trio context' bullet — 'Opus VIII deploys the full FFBE opening-arc party (Rain, Lasswell, Fina) simultaneously across multiple rarity slots'. Triple-source-corroborated, designer-formalized.
verification_log.graham_check: Graham 8-075C correctly excluded — Final Fantasy Dimensions (FFL is the Japanese title), not FFBE. Graham trivia explicitly distinguishes: 'Graham is a Dark Knight character from that game [FFDimensions], not from Final Fantasy Brave Exvius.'

next_pass_suggestions: FFDimensions cluster watch — Graham 8-075C confirmed FFDimensions; Sophia 8-038C from wave 135 was tagged 'FF Legends' and may also be FFDimensions. If Sophia verifies as FFDimensions, that's N=2 — still sub-threshold but worth a re-verification pass. Stage future ffdimensions-cohort node trigger condition: N>=3 FFDimensions characters in corpus.

FFIX cluster watch — Garnet 8-113C (just enriched) + Vivi 8-016H (existing vivi-ornitier node) = N=2 for FFIX / Category IX. Sub-threshold but same wave-134 pattern. Stage trigger condition: N>=3 FFIX characters in corpus, then evaluate either (a) ffix-party node analogous to ff6-returners-party, or (b) expand the existing vivi-ornitier node into a roster-cohort node.
Marlene EX 8-085C (FF7) — solo FF7 character; sub-threshold; future ff7-cohort watch.

FFXV co-headliner watch — Noctis EX 8-079H is the other co-headliner alongside Lasswell per the Opus VIII Square Enix press release; Ignis EX 8-072R is also in corpus (FFXV). N=2 for FFXV currently. Sub-threshold but the Opus VIII press release is the same designer-formalized debut anchor — when N=3 lands, this becomes a clean Nodeley pass via the same template.
other_candidates_considered: Edge: Rain 8-021R <-> Lasswell 8-043H (brothers-in-arms) — refused; subsumed by node
Edge: Fina 8-060L <-> Fina 8-061C (dual-print same-character) — already captured by both cards' Related cards; node makes it traversable
Edge: Rain 8-021R <-> Fina 8-061C (Vision-grantor / Vision-bearer) — refused; subsumed by node
